title: Uva-ursi intricata

The exact classification of Uva-ursi intricata may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered to be a type of bearberry. It is native to high-altitude and high-latitude regions, often growing in acidic soils and rocky areas.
uses: Uva-ursi intricata, also known as mountain cranberry, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. intro:
There is no plant species called "Uva-ursi intricata". However, I can provide information on the plant "Uva-ursi" (Arctostaphylos uva-ursi) or "Bearberry", which is a shrub native to the Northern Hemisphere.

Arctostaphylos uva-ursi, also known as Bearberry, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub with red berries that is native to acidic soils in northern regions. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to help prevent and treat urinary tract infections and kidney stones, due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Uva-ursi include arbutin, hydroquinone, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Uva-ursi include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to support urinary health and prevent infections.
